title: elaeocarpus tahanensis
intro: Elaeocarpus tahanensis, also known as the Tahan Tree, is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the Elaeocarpaceae family. It is native to Malaysia and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The extracts from this plant have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, providing health benefits such as pain relief and immune system support. Elaeocarpus tahanensis can be used in various herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are often made by combining the plant's extracts with other herbs and natural ingredients.
